_title: Fire-Rated Cables for Life Safety Circuits
_basis: BS 7671:2018 Reg 422 (Fire-resistant cables), BS 5839-1 (Fire alarm), BS 5266-1 (Emergency lighting), BS 8519 (Selection and installation of fire-resistant cables for life safety)
_note: Life safety circuits MUST use fire-rated cable that maintains circuit integrity during a fire for a defined period. Standard cable melts and short-circuits — life safety systems then fail when needed most.

always_required: Fire detection and alarm system (BS 5839-1) — wiring to call points, sounders, control panel
Emergency lighting (BS 5266-1) — supply to luminaires and central battery systems
Smoke control system (BS EN 12101) — supply to extract fans, dampers, control panel
Firefighter's lift (BS EN 81-72) — supply and control circuits
Wet riser pumps (BS 9990) — supply to firefighting water pumps
Sprinkler pumps (BS EN 12845) — supply to fire pump motors
Building public address (life safety messages) — system wiring
Emergency stop circuits in high-occupancy or process critical areas
may_be_required: BMS circuits controlling fire dampers or smoke vents
Lighting on protected escape routes (when not classed as 'emergency lighting')
Refuge alarm systems and disabled WC emergency calls
Security access control on escape routes (fail-safe should default to unlock)

iec_60331_11.description: Test method for fire-resistance of small-diameter cables (≤20mm). Flame temperature 750°C minimum.
iec_60331_11.typical_use: Standard fire alarm cables — FP200 Gold and equivalents.
iec_60331_11.duration: Minimum 90 minutes flame application + 15 min cooling.

iec_60331_21.description: Test method for larger cables (>20mm). Flame 750°C, mechanical shock applied.
iec_60331_21.typical_use: FP400, FP PLUS — larger cores for power circuits to fire pumps, smoke fans.

iec_60331_25.description: Higher rating — 950°C flame for 90 minutes plus mechanical shock and water spray.
iec_60331_25.typical_use: Cables in tunnels, deep basements, public transport.

bs_8434_1.description: UK national standard. Fire resistance under fire conditions with water and mechanical shock. PH 30, PH 60, PH 120 categories.
bs_8434_1.PH_30: 30 min fire resistance with shock and water — escape routes, short evac.
bs_8434_1.PH_60: 60 min — standard for most fire alarm systems in non-residential.
bs_8434_1.PH_120: 120 min — hospitals, high-rise residential post-Hackitt, critical infrastructure.

bs_en_50200.description: European standard for fire resistance of small cables. PH 15, PH 30, PH 60, PH 90, PH 120 categories.
bs_en_50200.harmonisation: BS 8434 maps to BS EN 50200 directly.

FP200_Gold.construction: Solid copper conductors, mica/glass tape barrier, LSZH insulation, aluminium foil screen, LSZH outer sheath, drain wire.
FP200_Gold.fire_rating: BS EN 50200 PH 30, BS 8434-1 PH 30 (60 min capability with appropriate clips per manufacturer)
FP200_Gold.typical_use: Fire alarm wiring (BS 5839-1), emergency lighting (BS 5266-1), in buildings with up to 30 minute escape time.
FP200_Gold.installation_notes: Use metallic fire-rated clips per manufacturer. Standard plastic clips will fail in fire and cause cable collapse — system loss.

FP400_PLUS.construction: Stranded copper, mica tape, LSZH insulation, foil screen, LSZH outer sheath, drain wire.
FP400_PLUS.typical_use: Sprinkler pumps, smoke extract fans, firefighter lift, voice alarm spine, public address backbone — circuits requiring 120 min survival.
FP400_PLUS.installation_notes: Heavier than FP200, requires more robust support. Larger cores allow longer voltage-drop-limited runs.

MICC_mineral_insulated.manufacturer: Various — Wrexham Mineral Cables, Tyco, MICA
MICC_mineral_insulated.construction: Solid copper conductors, magnesium oxide insulation, seamless copper outer sheath (sometimes LSZH-overjacketed).
MICC_mineral_insulated.fire_rating: BS 6387 CWZ — survives 950°C for 3 hours with water and impact. The highest-rated commonly available cable.
MICC_mineral_insulated.typical_use: Highest-risk applications — petrochemical, nuclear, military, deep tunnels, listed buildings where future re-cabling is impractical.
MICC_mineral_insulated.installation_notes: Specialist termination required (sealing pots and glands). Brittle if bent below minimum radius. Long lifespan (50+ years) — often justifies cost in critical infrastructure.

LSF_LSZH_standard_cable.construction: PVC or LSZH insulated and sheathed. Standard 70°C or 90°C rating.
LSF_LSZH_standard_cable.fire_rating: NONE — fails immediately in fire. Standard wiring cable.
LSF_LSZH_standard_cable.typical_use: All non-life-safety circuits. Lighting, power, sockets, data.
LSF_LSZH_standard_cable._note: Inclusion here to emphasise that this is NOT fire-rated and MUST NOT be used for life safety circuits even when the project is cost-pressured.

emergency_lighting_BS5266_1.standard: FP200 Gold or equivalent — circuit integrity for 60 min minimum
emergency_lighting_BS5266_1.central_battery_supply: FP400 PLUS for sub-circuits feeding multiple luminaires

smoke_extract_fans_BS_EN_12101.cable: FP400 PLUS or MICC. Size for motor full load current + 1.5× starting current.
smoke_extract_fans_BS_EN_12101._note: Specifically check motor protection coordination — fire-rated cable does not relax other BS 7671 requirements.

firefighter_lift_BS_EN_81_72.cable: FP400 PLUS or MICC for power, FP200 PLUS for control circuits

sprinkler_pumps_BS_EN_12845.duration_required: Minimum 60 min, 120 min for high-hazard occupancies
sprinkler_pumps_BS_EN_12845.cable: FP400 PLUS or MICC. Two independent feeds typically required.

voice_alarm_spine_BS_5839_8.duration_required: Per fire strategy — typically 60 to 120 min
voice_alarm_spine_BS_5839_8.cable: FP400 PLUS for spine, FP200 Gold for branch circuits

installation_requirements.cable_supports: Metallic fire-rated clips/saddles. Manufacturer-specified maximum spacing. Standard plastic clips FAIL in fire.
installation_requirements.containment: Where fire-rated cable runs in containment, the containment system must also have appropriate fire rating — typically metal trunking, conduit, or basket. Plastic containment will deform/melt and cause cable collapse.
installation_requirements.penetrations: Fire-stopped to maintain compartment integrity (BS EN 1366-3 tested seals).
installation_requirements.segregation: Fire-rated cables from different life safety systems should be segregated where possible — single fire event then cannot fail multiple systems.

supply_arrangements.rule: Life safety circuits should be supplied from the ESSENTIAL BUS — the section of the distribution system that remains energised when normal supply fails.
supply_arrangements.implementation: Essential bus is fed from generator and/or UPS via automatic transfer switch. SDB-EMRGY or similar designation.

common_pitfalls: Using FP200 Gold for circuits requiring PH 60 or PH 120 — non-compliant with fire strategy.
Mixing standard cable with fire-rated cable in the same containment — standard cable melts and damages fire-rated cable's outer sheath.
Standard plastic cable clips on fire-rated cable — cable collapses in fire and system fails. Use metal saddles only.
Routing life safety circuits through fire-risk areas without enhanced rating — duration of fire exposure exceeds cable's PH rating.
Specifying fire-rated cable but not fire-rated containment — system-level failure mode.
Sizing fire-rated cable on tabulated rating — same correction factors apply, fire rating does not change Iz.
